name: One-Way Platform
description: A platform that collides from selected directions while allowing entry or exit from others.
design_notes: One-way platforms are collision rules with player-facing expectations. They need consistent behavior for upward movement, downward drop-through, slopes, moving platforms, enemies, and projectiles.
design_purpose: Increase vertical route density without enclosing the player.
Let players jump through floors and land on top surfaces.
Support layered arena and platform layouts.
player_fantasy: The player feels they can move through platforms naturally while still trusting landings.

parameters.name: landing_normal_min_y
parameters.description: Minimum upward surface normal required for collision.

parameters.name: pass_through_margin
parameters.description: Tolerance used while entering from below.

parameters.name: drop_disable_ms
parameters.description: Window where collision is disabled after intentional drop-through.

edge_cases: Fast upward movement must not snag on the platform underside.
Moving one-way platforms need stable passenger transfer.
Characters starting inside the platform after spawn or load need a recovery rule.
common_bugs: Collision toggles globally and makes the platform non-solid for every actor.
The player lands from below because previous-frame position was above due to tunneling.
Drop-through re-enables before the player clears the platform thickness.
balancing_notes: Dense one-way layouts support vertical combat but can become visually confusing.
Drop-through delay should be long enough to avoid re-catching but short enough to permit quick recovery.
accessibility_notes: Use consistent visual language for semisolid platforms so players can identify collision behavior before jumping.

implementation_notes.unity: Use PlatformEffector2D where applicable or a custom collision filter that disables contact per actor during drop-through windows.
implementation_notes.godot: Use one-way collision settings for CollisionShape2D or explicit collision layers for 3D-style controllers.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement through custom movement collision filtering or platform components that ignore actors based on approach direction and drop state.
implementation_notes.web: Resolve platform collision only when the actor crosses the top plane from above and has not requested a drop-through window.
